title: Atalanta Fugiens
subtitle: Michael Maier's 50 Emblems with Epigrams, Discourses, and Fugues — Alchemical Multimedia from 1617

summary.one_line: The most sophisticated alchemical emblem book ever produced, combining 50 visual emblems, poetic epigrams, prose discourses, and musical fugues into a multimedia initiatory sequence.
summary.scope: Covers the emblematic structure of Atalanta Fugiens, with excerpts from selected emblems and their epigrams, focusing on the chase-and-catch of Atalanta/Hippomenes as the alchemical coniunctio, the musical structure of the fugues, and the discourse commentary.

sources.label: Atalanta Fugiens (Maier 1617), emblems 1-50 transcribed from Sloane MS 3645, with modern translations by Clay Holden, Hereward Tilton, and Peter Branwin

body.text: [The full text of the emblems is in atl1-5.html through atl46-50.html. The central myth: Atalanta (the volatile, the fugitive) is caught by Hippomenes (the fixed) who throws golden apples (temptations, weights) to slow her flight. Emblem 1 epigram:] Let her fly, but she will be caught; / The golden apples are thrown; / The fleet-footed one is detained; / The conqueror carries off the prize.

[Maier's innovation: each of the 50 emblems is accompanied by a three-part musical fugue. The term 'fugiens' means both 'fleeing' (as in the myth) and 'fugue' (musical form). The voices chase each other like Atalanta and Hippomenes. The musical notation is arranged so that one voice flees and the other pursues, exactly mirroring the alchemical operation of volatile chasing fixed.]

[Emblem 7: A dragon (ouroboros) biting its tail, standing in a circle of flames. Epigram:] The Dragon dies not save by his own brother, / And by his sister he is slain; / These two are born of one mother / And yet hate each other with deadly pain. / For the brother kills the sister / And the sister slays the brother; / Yet from their death springs forth another — / The Son who lives forever.

[Emblem 21: A double-headed hermaphrodite figure standing on a dragon, holding a cup in one hand and a caduceus in the other. Epigram:] Behold the Hermaphrodite, born of two, / Who is both male and female in one body; / Without him no true work can be done; / He is the secret Stone, the hidden Key / That opens the locks of the Philosopher's treasury.

The three-part fugue structure of Atalanta Fugiens mirrors the three nāḍīs: the right voice (Hippomenes/fixed) is piṅgalā (sun, fixed, fire), the left voice (Atalanta/fleeing) is iḍā (moon, volatile, water), and the third voice (the bassus, the ground) is suṣumṇā (the central channel, the neutral fire). The chase and catch is the circuit of prāṇa: iḍā and piṅgalā alternate their flow, circling around suṣumṇā, until they meet at ajñā-cakra and conjoin. The 'golden apples' that slow Atalanta correspond to the bindu-drop that must be stabilized in the body — the drops of amṛta that fall from bindu-viśuddhi and must be caught (prevented from descending into maṇipūra to be burnt). When the bindu is fixed, the volatile prāṇa is 'caught' and the coniunctio is accomplished.

The ouroboros (Emblem 7) — the dragon that kills itself and is reborn — is Steiner's image of the purified astral body. In Steiner's 'Knowledge of Higher Worlds', the astral body must be cleansed from its chaotic desires (the 'fiery dragon') and transformed into the 'soul body' or 'astral sheath' that can perceive the supersensible world. The brother killing sister then birthing 'the Son who lives forever' is Steiner's description of the three stages: the soul divests itself of the lower astral (death of the brother), the lower etheric (death of the sister), and from these deaths emerges the Spirit Self (the Son), which is the 'I' that lives eternally. The dragon in flames is precisely the fire of the heart (anāhata) that must consume the dross of desire before the spiritual heart can open.

The hermaphrodite (Emblem 21) — the Rebis, the 'two-made-one' — is the direct symbol of the HGA contact from the daimon path perspective. In the Abramelin operation, the final stage is the 'conversation with the HGA', where the magician and the Angel become 'one body, two souls' — exactly the androgyne condition. The two heads looking in opposite directions represent the dual awareness: the practitioner in the world and the HGA in the divine realm, but occupying the same body ('one body'). The caduceus in one hand and cup in the other represent respectively the twin serpents (iḍā/piṅgalā) and the Grail cup (the heart that holds the mingled essence). In Tantraloka (IV.198-205), Abhinavagupta teaches that the union of Śiva and Śakti within the body produces the 'third' — the undivided awareness (turīya) that is neither male nor female.

The Atalanta-Hippomenes myth as interpreted by Maier parallels the PGM's 'Meeting with Your Own Daimon' (PGM VII.505-528) in a striking way. The 'chase' is the magician's pursuit of the daimon, which is volatile and flees — the daimon is 'Atalanta', the 'fugitive.' The 'golden apples' are the barbarous names and ritual actions that weigh down and capture the daimonic presence. In the PGM ritual, the egg is the 'golden apple' — the coated body that attracts the daimon. The three-fugue structure corresponds to the three stages of the PGM rite: Skinning (purification), Egg (charging), and Communion (the union). Hippomenes' three golden apples from Aphrodite are the three invocations that slow the daimon enough to be caught. The race that is won not by speed but by cunning reflects the theurgic principle of Iamblichus: the gods/daimons cannot be compelled but can be attracted by sympathetic means — 'like seduces like.'

gaps: Only 4 of 50 emblems excerpted — the full set of 50 is needed for comprehensive coverage
Musical analysis of fugue structure referencing specific scores not yet performed

issues.title: Only 4 of 50 emblems excerpted
issues.description: Atalanta Fugiens contains 50 emblems, each with epigram, discourse, and fugue. Only emblems 1, 7, 21 are directly quoted, plus general description of the fugue structure.

issues.title: The fugues are not yet musically analyzed
issues.description: Maier included actual musical notation. A proper analysis would examine the counterpoint, mode, and structure as it relates to the alchemical operation.

versions.summary: Initial RO covering selected emblems (1, 7, 21) and the fugue structure of Atalanta Fugiens.

timeline.event: RO created in draft state from Atalanta Fugiens source
